economic_notes: Sioux Valley Dakota Nation achieved self-government status on July 1, 2014, with recognized jurisdiction in over 50 areas. Nation-to-Nation relationship with Canada and Manitoba. Operates gaming (Sioux Valley Gaming Centre), retail (Dakota Quickstop, Dakota Valley Gas Mart), lodge (Sioux Valley Dakota Oyate Lodge), and housing entities.
notes: Fiscal year ended March 31, 2023 (not 2024). Province is Manitoba (MB). Consolidates 14 entities including gaming, retail fuel, gas bar, lodge, housing, and development corporations. Prior year comparatives restated per Note 23 (not included in extracted text). Capital schedule shows large infrastructure and capital spending. Obligations under capital lease of $397,723 exist in addition to long-term debt. Annual debt service figure represents repayment of long-term debt only ($1,127,014); capital lease repayments were an additional $257,531. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($46,409,564) + CMHC ($1,242,489) + Southern First Nations Network of Care ($3,032,507) + Health Canada ($30,810) + Government of Manitoba ($75,196) = $50,790,566. No schedule of remuneration was included in the extracted text. Economic development expenses of $18,324,432 include a transfer to 6559710 MB Inc of $2,382,068 and are largely driven by consolidated retail/enterprise operations.
